title: GUILD competitive positioning in agent-native design orchestration
objective: decide GUILD's positioning: is there a direct competitor, and should GUILD compete on generation or on discipline-specialized design orchestration?
context: GUILD is an 8-persona design guild (research/IA/interaction/visual/content/QA/ops/design-system) with a hard Sage GO/NO-GO gate, brownfield traceability spine, multi-model raids, and Deep Research expeditions, running natively inside AI coding agents and integrating with BMAD.
questions: Does any tool orchestrate multiple discipline-specialized DESIGN personas through a full research-to-handoff pipeline?
Which tools span the full UX research -> IA -> interaction -> visual -> content -> QA -> handoff lifecycle vs only a slice?
Are there open-source repos templated specifically for design workflows inside AI coding agents?
Is there a real IDE-native / agent-native design trend, and what form does it take?
Who are the closest comparables, and do we compete / complement / integrate with each?
Do GUILD's Raid (multi-model) and Expedition (multi-Deep-Research reconcile) mechanics have any first-party equivalent?
What are the pricing, business model, and adoption signals of the closest comparables?
must_cover: v0 / Lovable / Bolt / Builder Fusion / Subframe / Magic Patterns
hard design QA gate as differentiator
sources_guidance: Prefer primary sources and material from the last 18 months; cite every claim.
